mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations. A sustained downward trend in FEDFUNDS over the preceding 3 months signals monetary easing that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of Feb 2007 reflects recent Fed tightening cycle
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which compresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points in 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.2% as of Feb 2007, near cyclical lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) directly reflect residential investment demand and forward consumer wealth expectations. Declining housing starts signal weakening demand and reduced future consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward-looking equity markets price in demand contraction.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1534k units in Jan 2007, elevated but showing early signs of deceleration
regime_hint: housing cycle peak with demand deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite HOUST decline signal, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real economic output capacity utilization. Sustained month-over-month growth in INDPRO signals robust supply-side expansion and pricing power, supporting corporate profit margins and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.59 in Feb 2007, near cyclical peak
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong supply utilization
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during INDPRO growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly capture consumer and residential demand momentum. When both series decline simultaneously, it signals broad-based demand weakness across consumer and investment channels, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. The interaction amplifies the signal beyond either series alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month in same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 617.7B in Jan 2007, HOUST at 1534k, both showing early weakness
regime_hint: demand-side recession onset with synchronized contraction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 70% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of forward returns on RSAFS and HOUST declines.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly characterize labor market tightness. When PAYEMS growth slows below 100k/month while UNRATE rises, it signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer income and spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 50k AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 147.19M in Feb 2007, UNRATE at 5.2%, labor market still tight
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market inflection with growth deceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of co-triggered instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 2.5% year-over-year in a regime where FEDFUNDS remains above 5% signals monetary policy constraint and real rate pressure. This regime produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as markets price in sustained policy tightness and margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 273.01 in Feb 2007, FEDFUNDS at 5.25%, real rates elevated
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation persistence
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances where both conditions are met, or the interaction coefficient fails to reach statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly captures aggregate demand trajectory. In late-cycle expansions (GDPC1 growth slowing below 2% annualized), further deceleration signals demand exhaustion and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19360.6B in Q4 2006, growth moderating from cycle peak
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with growth deceleration and demand exhaustion
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns following sub-2% GDPC1 growth quarters are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
